Historical Trades

Option Trade Archive

Past trade ideas published on the Forecast page — with outcomes, P/L, and lessons learned.

This archive tracks trade ideas originally published on the Option Trade Ideas for Today page. Each entry shows the spread structure, entry rationale, and outcome when closed. 📊 Updated daily.

Last updated: April 14, 2026

🟢 CLOSED

Bull Put Spread — SPY

April 10, 2026 · Expired Apr 17, 2026 · 7 DTE

Net P/L
+$150/contract
Credit collected
Strategy Bull Put Spread (2 legs)
Short Strike SPY $672 put — sold for credit
Long Strike SPY $667 put — protection
Net Credit ~$1.50/sh ($150/contract)
Underlying SPY
Lesson: Bull put spreads work well in sideways-to-slightly-bullish VIX environments. Max risk is the width of the spread minus credit received.
🟢 CLOSED

Iron Condor — SPY

April 10, 2026 · Expired Apr 17, 2026 · 8 DTE

Net P/L
+$130/contract
Credit collected
Strategy Iron Condor (4 legs)
Net Credit ~$1.30/sh ($130/contract)
Leg 1 SPY $668 put (short, upper put wing)
Leg 2 SPY $663 put (long, lower put wing)
Leg 3 SPY $688 call (short, upper call wing)
Leg 4 SPY $693 call (long, upper call wing)
Underlying SPY
Lesson: Iron condors shine when VIX is elevated (18+) and the market has a clear range. The key risk is a gap move through one of the wings.
Disclaimer: Trade entries reflect research published on the Forecast page and are for educational purposes only. Options trading involves significant risk of loss. Past performance does not indicate future results. Not investment advice.
